snippets.description: POST /staff-invite/start's liveness guard — CURRENT shape (pre-ADM-013). The select projection (2 fields, plus email) and the 2-arm OR-guard ADM-013 must extend to a 3rd arm (revokedAt !== null). This is site #1 of the 4 liveness-check sites the SPEC's headline correctness invariant requires.

POST /staff-invite/consume's atomic compare-and-swap claim — CURRENT shape (post-ADM-012 CodeRabbit fix, commit 188b9c8). CRITICAL: this already has 4 and()-ed conditions (not the 3 the plan's original prose assumed) — the 4th, eq(staffInvites.email, req.user!.email), was added by ADM-012 to prevent a session/invite email mismatch from claiming someone else's invite. ADM-013's isNull(revokedAt) must be added as a 5th condition, not a 4th (Execute-Agent Instruction E5). This is site #2 of the 4 liveness-check sites.

POST /invite's create-time supersede predicate (inside db.transaction) — CURRENT shape. This is site #3 of the 4 liveness-check sites: the supersede UPDATE currently matches on 3 conditions (email, isNull(consumedAt), gt(expiresAt, now)); ADM-013 must add isNull(revokedAt) as a 4th so a revoked invite for the same email is never re-marked consumed by a fresh create. Also shows the super_admin-only guard-first pattern (line 211) the 3 new ADM-013 routes must replicate, and confirms export default staffRouter is the file's final line (299) — the append point for the 3 new handlers.

roleUpdateSchema (already accepts 'customer') and POST /api/admin/users/:id/role's full guard order — the EXISTING route Part B reuses byte-identical, zero modification. Confirms Part B's core feasibility claim: the self-modification guard (line 63) runs before Zod parsing and checks only req.params.id === req.adminSession!.userId (target-role-agnostic), so self-demotion is already blocked with zero new code, and the super_admin-only check (line 57) already covers a demotion caller the same way it covers a promotion caller.

The staff_invites table ADM-013's migration 0021 adds revoked_at to. Confirms the additive-only nature of the change (no existing column touched) and the existing token-hash-at-rest / single-live-invite-per-email design ADM-013's revoke/resend routes must stay consistent with — no revokedAt column exists yet in this file, confirming migration 0021 is genuinely additive against the current schema.
snippets.content:
export const staffInvites = pgTable('staff_invites', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  email: varchar('email').notNull(),
  intendedRole: userRoleEnum('intended_role').notNull(),
  intendedBranchId: uuid('intended_branch_id').references(() => branches.id),
  tokenHash: varchar('token_hash').notNull(),
  expiresAt: timestamp('expires_at').notNull(),
  consumedAt: timestamp('consumed_at'),
  createdBy: uuid('created_by')
    .references(() => users.id)
    .notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow().notNull(),
});
